name: Pigmeat Markham
abstract: Dewey "Pigmeat" Markham (April 18, 1904 – December 13, 1981) was an American entertainer. Though best known as a comedian, Markham was also a singer, dancer, and actor.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Jazz Stars at Midnight

about_quotes.speaker: Nellie Lutcher
about_quotes.evidence: I can remember Bard u Ali , who's out here, and Pigmeat Markham. They came out here from New York... he used to sort of produce the shows at the Lincoln Theater.

about_quotes.speaker: Alvin Queen
about_quotes.evidence: I saw Pigmeat Markham, I saw Redd Foxx, I saw Michael Jackson when he was five years old, I saw Otis Redding. That’s who I used to see.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alvin Queen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, August 23, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Sammy Price
about_quotes.evidence: But they always had to have that original TOBA format of a comedian, Pigmeat Markham, you know what I mean, Pigmeat, Bert Williams

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: And the old timers like Pigmeat, Pigmeat Markham, Dusty Fletcher. Gee whiz, I can't think of the rest of them.

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Tate
about_quotes.evidence: I think Pigmeat was with one of his shows... I know he remembers him very well, because I've asked him

about_quotes.speaker: Zilner T. Randolph
about_quotes.evidence: I got with the show playing -- leading the band with a little show starring Pigmeat Markham.
